---
title: "Tau Mutant Integration"
author: "Emir Turkes"
format:
  html:
    theme: theme.scss        # crimson primary + links + IBM Plex (Sans body, Serif headings, Mono code; inlined offline)
    embed-resources: true    # inline CSS/JS -> ONE self-contained offline HTML file
    lightbox: auto           # dense figures are clickable in the offline HTML
    toc: true
    code-fold: true          # analysis code present but collapsed (v1's code_folding: hide)
    code-tools: true
---

# Overview

This report presents an integrative analysis of single-nucleus RNA-seq, GeoMx spatial
transcriptomics, and bulk proteomics and phosphoproteomics across four mouse models of
Alzheimer's disease. It asks how amyloid reshapes microglia under different tau
backgrounds, reading the tau-amyloid interaction across all four modalities.

The synthesis comes first because the rebuilt answer is compact: amyloid drives a
homeostatic-to-DAM microglial programme; mutant tau modulates that response mainly as extra
DAM-cell composition rather than supported further trajectory progression; Myc has focused RNA
support; NF-kB attenuation and Gsk3b are not recovered; SpatialDecon abundance is blocked and full
CCC is not called. The remaining chapters are the audit trail. QC checks the modality shapes and the
single-nucleus genotype-by-batch design, then the microglia, trajectory, mechanism and
cross-modality chapters show the evidence behind each synthesis row.

The analysis is built on a reproducible spine: a pinned R environment (rv), a pinned Python
environment (uv), a targets pipeline, and this single self-contained Quarto report.

{{< include _synthesis.qmd >}}

{{< include _qc.qmd >}}

{{< include _microglia.qmd >}}

{{< include _trajectory.qmd >}}

{{< include _mechanism.qmd >}}

{{< include _crossmodality.qmd >}}
